item.title: Business Brief: How oil makes and moves the world
item.description: The many uses of crude means changing prices are felt widely

item.title: ‘A last-ditch effort to just get better’: Can chatbots replace therapists?
item.description: The fourth episode of our podcast series examines how AI is being used for emotional support and therapy.

item.title: FirstFT: The battle for the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Democratic Party rising star James Talarico wins primary and Howard Lutnick agrees to appear before committee over Epstein links

item.title: Citi’s security unit was meant to probe misconduct. Employees say it protected the bank instead
item.description: Internal team staffed by ex-law enforcement ‘operates as HR’s internal hit squad’, former executive says

item.title: Can Big Tech deliver on Trump’s pledge to shield consumers from higher energy bills?
item.description: US president pushes data centre operators to build their own power supplies as AI demand adds to costs

item.title: Spellbook secures US$40-million from RBC to snap up smaller legal AI startups
item.description: St. John’s company emerging as industry leader in legal AI contracting software with US$100-million revenue in sights

item.title: Dassault warns Franco-German jet project is ‘dead’ if Airbus refuses to co-operate
item.description: Chief executive Éric Trappier blames German arm of Airbus for blocking progress

item.title: Markets calmer after Trump vows to protect oil and gas ships at the Hormuz chokepoint
item.description: Israel pledges to assassinate Iran’s next leader, who could be Ali Khamenei’s son Mojtaba

item.title: Markets today: Wall Street futures rise after report on Iran-U.S. talks to end war
item.description: Oil shock fear hits Asian tech stocks while European selloff pauses

item.title: Luigi Lovaglio set to be ousted as Monte dei Paschi chief
item.description: Incumbent left off list of candidates for new board ahead of his current term ending next month

item.title: Millions face longer waits for payouts from £11bn UK motor finance redress scheme
item.description: Regulator grants lenders extra time to prepare for record compensation plan

item.description: Regulator grants lenders extra time to prepare for record compensation plan

item.title: Dubai evacuation costs rise as high as $250,000 as more families flee
item.description: Increasing number of expats and employees are deciding to leave the city as regional conflict escalates

item.title: South Korean stocks hit hardest by Iran war as market plunges 12%
item.description: Kospi index suffers worst one-day drop as conflict rocks Asian markets

item.title: Adidas taps billionaire Nassef Sawiris for chair
item.description: Sportswear group plans to elevate deputy chair and extends tenure of chief Bjørn Gulden to 2030

item.title: You probably can’t afford to visit the Moon, sorry

item.title: The cynical opportunities of ‘Epic Fury’
item.description: With this US administration, the best bet is that a business deal is to be reached with IranMartin Wolf

item.description: New state-backed body seeks AI breakthroughs in science, healthcare and transport

item.description: As groups watch their shares plunge, they’ve been forced to explain their portfolios and prove their assets’ worth

item.description: British gas and electricity markets are more sensitive than most other European states to supply disruptions from Middle East

item.title: Formula 1’s pivotal year
item.description: The motor racing series has new rules, new teams and a new US broadcaster in Apple. Will it be a recipe for further growth?

item.description: Attacks in the Strait of Hormuz echo the 1980s conflict. Here are the weapons Tehran can use against commercial shipping

item.description: Publicly traded vehicles are trading at steep discounts in gloomy sign for broader industry

item.title: Stamp duty is Britain’s quiet growth killer
item.description: The rate of housebuilding has plummeted over decades; to achieve recovery, the government needs to scrap high transaction taxesTax

item.title: Should I marry my partner to protect me financially?
item.description: He is terminally ill and I’m worried my child and I won’t be able to stay in our home when he is goneTax

item.description: Business-to-business services are bread and butter for a lot of investors

item.title: China to lean on Russian oil as Iran crisis chokes supply
item.description: Energy disruption set to force Xi Jinping closer to Vladimir Putin and to consider drawing on reserves

item.title: Ottawa announces more than $100-million in funding for electricity push in Western Canada
item.description: Two projects in B.C. and one in Saskatchewan will get the boost as the government looks to ramp up electricity strategy

item.title: US shale bosses warn they cannot replace war-hit Middle East oil
item.description: New production from Texas will take months as drillers use windfall from price rise to pay investors

item.title: Pro-Iran ‘hacktivists’ could target Canadian organizations, experts warn
item.description: Political activists, journalists and human rights advocates also likely to be targeted, the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security says

item.title: AGT Food and Ingredients stock stumbles on first day of trading
item.description: Agrifood giant’s shares shed 7.6 per cent value as company raises $450-million in IPO

item.title: War in the Middle East makes the case for more Canadian energy
item.description: Iran war is yet another reminder of why Canada must prioritize long-term projects that increase energy security

item.title: Submarine bidders open to Ottawa splitting contract, say procurement speed setting new standard
item.description: Ottawa’s economic approach to contract could influence similar deals in the future, CEO of German submarine maker TKMS says

item.title: Energy markets face global supply shock as Middle East war widens
item.description: If necessary, President Trump says U.S. Navy will escort tankers through Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Telus planning to offer direct-to-cell satellite services in late 2026
item.description: Telecom takes equity stake in Texan satellite manufacturer AST SpaceMobile as part of commercial agreement

item.title: Israel vows to kill next Iranian leader
item.description: Spain’s prime minister brands war ‘disaster’ in defiant response to Donald Trump’s criticism

item.title: Fertility rates are dropping, but not among older women – and that creates a ripple effect
item.description: Today, the birth rate for 37-year-old women is near triple what it was in 1977

item.title: BCE Inc. to modernize three federal call centres
item.description: Telecoms company will team up with Genesys Canada to improve government’s client services by implementing AI and digital tools

item.title: Intel board chair Frank Yeary to retire after May annual meeting
item.description: Yeary will be replaced by Craig Barratt in a major shakeup for the struggling U.S. chipmaker’s board

item.description: Oil briefly hit $85 a barrel as Iraqi production shuts down because so few tankers risk journey through Strait of Hormuz

item.description: They win only if governments want just enough weapons—but not too many

item.description: Can the emirate keep people and investors coming?

item.description: Online travel agent was tipped to be London Stock Exchange’s first significant listing of 2026

item.title: US petrol prices surge as Trump’s Iran war triggers inflation worries
item.description: Costs at the pump exceed level at the end of the Biden administration

item.title: Roots launches strategic review, including possible sale of retailer
item.description: Company says its board will analyze and evaluate a range of alternatives

item.title: Thousands of travellers left stranded in the Middle East as Iran war complicates routes home
item.description: More than 19,000 of roughly 51,600 flights have been cancelled, according to aviation analytics firm Cirium

item.description: U.S. bank beefing up operations to better serve one of the world’s top 10 innovation markets

item.title: To Trump’s tariffs and chaos, world-travelling Carney says ‘not today’
item.description: Prime Minister’s foreign trips rallying new trade allies to our side signals he is preparing for a necessary trade battle with the U.S.

item.title: India has an oil problem. The Iran war makes it worse. Canada is the solution
item.description: Building additional capacity to tidewater secures Canadian jobs, strengthens democratic alliances – and positions Canada to lead as an exporter of solutions

item.title: U.S. travel software firm adopts poison pill, thwarting further investment by Constellation
item.description: Toronto-based Constellation recently acquired 9.7-per-cent stake in Texas-based Sabre

item.title: Hampton Securities CEO Peter Deeb appeals ruling by securities regulator that includes industry suspension
item.description: Panel determined last April he had engaged in trading practices that were ‘a serious violation of CIRO’s rules’

item.title: Canada’s submarine conundrum: Buying a few boats is harder than it looks
item.description: Ottawa will have to perform a delicate balancing act between learning from procurement mistakes, pivoting to new supply chains and meeting North American alliance commitments

item.title: Airlines take a hit from hostilities in the Middle East
item.description: The war will hurt the Gulf carriers—and the whole industry too

item.title: Ontario says it will complete construction on roads to the Ring of Fire by 2031
item.description: Completion of the road network will be five years ahead of schedule, Premier Doug Ford says

item.title: Canadian manufacturing PMI rises to 13-month high in February as new business increased
item.description: S&P Global Canada Manufacturing Purchasing Managers’ Index, a measure of factory sector health, rose to 51.0 last month from 50.4 in January

item.title: Former Competition Bureau chief Matthew Boswell to join Norton Rose Fulbright
item.description: He oversaw the agency’s unsuccessful bid to stop Rogers’s $20-billion takeover of Shaw, which preceded some changes in competition law

item.title: Trump ponders Plans B to D after his favourite tariffs are taken away
item.description: The replacement duties for those ruled illegal by the Supreme Court may be less vulnerable to legal challengeAlan Beattie

item.title: Can A.I. Be Pro-Worker?
item.description: As fears of mass unemployment grow, three leading economists advocate some policies to shift the focus from job displacement to job enhancement.

item.title: Ottawa says provinces should pay for nurse practitioners, gives one-year grace before penalties
item.description: New policy amounts to one of the most significant updates of the Canada Health Act in years

item.title: OpenAI has shown it cannot be trusted. Canada needs nationalized, public AI
item.description: OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT, lobbied Ottawa for business. All the while it hid its knowledge of Tumbler Ridge shooter

item.description: One of which is Donald Trump

item.title: War in Iran could cause the biggest oil shock in years
item.description: Prices have surged—and may stay high for a while

item.title: Britain’s rising tax on ambition
item.description: The country’s tax system and rising costs sap the returns to hard workTax
